predecessor_evidence.finding: Round 21 revision 1 canonical report 2fcad6c998d169c4bcaa540be0b0edab7e11c325e9a29b4f7ee15f1ea2500a99 is excluded because DirectML reported an aten::roll CPU fallback, violating the GPU-native experiment contract. Revision 2 preserves every economic and model setting while using DirectML-supported concatenation for cyclic pair sampling and making CPU-fallback warnings test failures.

research_basis.title: Strictly Proper Scoring Rules, Prediction, and Estimation
research_basis.use: Supports the retained unweighted binary log loss for truthful profitability probabilities. The separate ranking term remains explicitly non-proper, so Brier diagnostics continue to expose calibration damage.

research_basis.title: Deep Learning for Limit Order Books
research_basis.use: Motivates modeling a conditional future distribution and inspecting its tails, while this experiment explicitly remains limited to observed best-bid/offer and trade features.

research_basis.title: DeepLOB: Deep Convolutional Neural Networks for Limit Order Books
research_basis.use: Supports shared nonlinear representations for order-book prediction but does not establish an after-cost edge for this model or corpus.

research_basis.title: BDLOB: Bayesian Deep Convolutional Neural Networks for Limit Order Books
research_basis.use: Motivates independent-seed dispersion and abstention when predictive uncertainty is high rather than forcing a trade.

research_basis.title: The Price Impact of Order Book Events
research_basis.use: Finds that short-horizon price impact varies with order-flow imbalance and inversely with depth. Round 19 adds causal depth-normalized aggressive order-flow inputs based on opposing displayed L1 depth; profitability remains unproven.

research_basis.title: Order Flows and Limit Order Book Resiliency on the Meso-Scale
research_basis.use: Supports combining active trade flow with passive depth and resiliency rather than treating directional trade imbalance as sufficient on its own.

research_basis.title: Deep Learning for Digital Asset Limit Order Books
research_basis.use: Provides crypto-specific evidence that short-horizon order-book prediction is testable with walk-forward data, without implying execution profitability.

research_basis.title: Deep Limit Order Book Forecasting
research_basis.use: Shows that strong limit-order-book forecast metrics need not produce actionable transactions. Round 20 therefore retains exact after-cost threshold selection and out-of-sample risk controls rather than treating forecast discrimination as profitability.

research_basis.title: Price Discovery in Bitcoin: The Role of Limit Orders
research_basis.use: Reports directional asymmetry in permanent Bitcoin price impact and material dependence on order-book state. This motivates testing separate long and short representations without importing the paper's findings as model validation.

research_basis.title: The Adaptive Nature of Liquidity Taking in Limit Order Books
research_basis.use: Documents direction-dependent interactions among order-flow predictability, opposing liquidity, and price impact. Round 20 tests whether one shared representation causes harmful coupling between directional outcomes.

research_basis.title: Gradient Surgery for Multi-Task Learning
research_basis.use: Establishes that conflicting task gradients can cause destructive interference in shared multi-task models. Independent side towers are the smallest falsifiable separation experiment; no benefit is presumed.

research_basis.title: Conflict-Averse Gradient Descent for Multi-task Learning
research_basis.use: Shows that average-loss optimization can underperform independently learned tasks when gradients conflict. Round 20 holds losses and training capacity effectively constant while removing shared long/short parameters.

research_basis.title: Learning to Rank using Gradient Descent
research_basis.use: Introduces RankNet's probabilistic pairwise ranking loss. Round 21 applies its ordering principle to sampled pairs of realized net returns while retaining proper probability and conditional-magnitude losses.

research_basis.title: Building Cross-Sectional Systematic Strategies By Learning to Rank
research_basis.use: Provides finance-specific evidence that direct ranking objectives can differ materially from sorting ordinary regression outputs. Its cross-sectional results are motivation only and do not validate this single-instrument high-frequency experiment.

research_basis.title: Do Order-Book States Predict Passive-Buy Toxicity? Evidence from BTC Perpetual Futures
research_basis.use: Recent non-peer-reviewed BTC evidence motivates testing depth-normalized aggressive order flow, near-touch displayed liquidity, and liquidity fragility; its reported findings are not imported as validation.

research_basis.title: DirectML
research_basis.use: Defines the vendor-neutral DirectX 12 training path used in a clean process on this AMD host. Revision 2 promotes any DirectML CPU-fallback warning to an exception during training and inference.
